sections.title: Attempting to open a pot of bone seeds near Malignius Mortifer

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You should take that pot a bit further away from me before you open it!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: So, [player name], your curiosity leads you to speak to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About those enchanted secateurs again...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: What now, [player name], can't you just leave me alone to practice my art?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get another set of enchanted secateurs?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Well I guess it's your lucky day! Since your last visit I've been thinking, I never use my enchanted secateurs anymore, I could sell them to you, if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much would they cost?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I could let you have them for a mere 40,000 gp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 40,000! That's outrageous!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Well, that's my price. If you change your mind, you can come back and talk to me. Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's too expensive for me I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Well, that's my price. If you change your mind, you can come back and talk to me. Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, great.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: A pleasure doing business with you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You don't have room to hold them. Stop wasting my time.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You don't have 40,000 gp with you! Do you take me for a fool?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I didn't realise. I'll have to visit a bank.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Fine, I might see you later then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry to have bothered you.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: That's ok, I shouldn't have been so abrupt with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you spare me a brown apron?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Oh, if you must.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have some more bone seeds?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You fetch me a pot with a lid first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fortunately I do have another pot with a lid.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Yes, yes, have fun with them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you and what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I am the great Malignius Mortifer, wielder of strange and terrible powers. These lowly followers of mine are dedicated students of the magical arts. Their business is to follow me and learn all they can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They don't look very tough.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: You may believe that, but even if you strike one down, another will rise up within minutes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, right.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Each of my followers is a master of his chosen element. His life becomes bound to that element in a way you could not hope to understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do you do?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I am mastering a branch of magic that few dare to attempt: Necromancy! The fools in the Guild of Wizards shun anyone who practices this art, but there are a few in this land who know the rudiments.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Grayzag, Invrigar, Sithik... Even Melzar studied the methods of necromancy, until an accident affected his mind. Now his spells tend to result in...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Grayzag and Invrigar... Even Melzar studied the methods of necromancy, until an accident affected his mind. Now his spells tend to result in...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: ... well, let us simply say that he does NOT raise armies of undead minions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this like Summoning? Can you teach me?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: The obscure magical art of necromancy has nothing to do with Summoning.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Through necromancy, I may resurrect the bodies of those who have fallen, and bend them to my will, and even command them to tell me hidden knowledge.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I shall not pass on my secrets lightly to any passer-by!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even a mighty hero like me?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Perhaps one day you shall learn ways to summon creatures to your side, but my own work is of a different sort.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Deluded lover of Guthix, you will get no secrets from me!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Saradominist fool, you will get no secrets from me!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Although I note your respect for our Lord Zamorak, I am afraid the subtle secrets of necromancy must remain beyond your grasp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to Melzar?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: The forces required to wield power over the dead are very difficult to control. Melzar's concentration slipped, and his spell rebounded upon him.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Although he did not die, his mind broke. Now he dwells amidst the beasts that fill his strange home, and when he attempts to do magic he gets... amusing results.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you keep making fungi appear?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Necromancy is... still not fully understood. So few people dare to study it. Although my spells usually raise whichever undead creature I desire, they sometimes misfire. As Melzar has learnt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll be off now.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I am sure your curiosity will eventually draw you to speak to me again, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teach me something about magic?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I see no glimmer of understanding in your eyes; my time would be wasted trying to teach you of the subtle arts.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: If you are truly interested in magic, perhaps you should go and speak to the Duke of Lumbridge, who lives east of this place. Perchance he will have a mission for you that will teach you something.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Ah, you are an inquisitive fellow. I shall speak of the great Wizards' Tower, destroyed by fire many years ago...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Many say it was the greatest building in the history of Gielinor, a magnificent monument to human ingenuity.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Yet when humans are offered great power, they so often buy it at the cost of their principles.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Wizards who claimed allegiance to Saradomin began to insist that magic be restricted to the few they deemed 'worthy' of such powers.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Before long, those who did not share their fatuous obsession with Saradomin were excluded from the Tower completely. This state of affairs could not continue.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: One dark night, a brave band of magi loyal to the great Lord Zamorak stormed the Tower. Despite heavy losses, they fought their way up the levels until they reached the Great Library.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Alas, they were trapped and could not steal the precious records. But before they could be defeated, the bravest of them took it upon himself to blast the writings into nothingness, destroying the library.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: The ensuing fire raced through the tower, and none escaped its fury. Some say that Lord Zamorak himself fanned the flames to ensure the destruction of his enemies!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Much of that knowledge was lost, and some has never yet been rediscovered. Still, your efforts on behalf of my colleagues have not been wasted! Once again, the forces of Zamorak can create unlimited runestones!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: May the world cower in its boots!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: I bid you farewell for now.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Much of that knowledge was lost, and some has never yet been rediscovered. Still, the power of magic remains in the world, and we who dedicate our lives to its study may yet perform great works...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll be going now.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About those skeletal magi in the Colony - why don't they disappear now the Colony's safe?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: HAHAHA! I never specified how long they'd stay there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So when will they go away? They're in the dormitories, and that's a real nuisance for the colonists!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're not going to get rid of them?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, forget I asked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get clothes like those?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Bah! Our garments are an outward sign of our dominance of the magical arts. You cannot simply buy them in a shop!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens if I kill you and take them?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Try it and see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about if you teach me enough about magic so I can wear those clothes too?

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: How about if I turn you into a mushroom to make you stop bothering me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: MMMmmph!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't want to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Bah! Then go away!

sections.title: When attempting to summon a creature

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Da mihi potentiam!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Veni, cohortes malefici!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Curses - more fungi!

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Blast - fungi again!

lines.speaker: Zombie
lines.text: DUUUHHH

lines.speaker: Ghost
lines.text: OooOOOooooOOOOooo

lines.speaker: Skeleton mage
lines.text: Krrrrsssss

lines.speaker: Skeleton
lines.text: Krrrrsssss

lines.speaker: Malignius Mortifer
lines.text: Oi - stop waving your items at me!
